title: Layers of the Earth (Crust, Mantle, Core)

summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Layers of the Earth (Crust, Mantle, Core)
summary_sections.content_html:
Earth's Internal Structure

Layers by Composition

  • Crust: Thin outermost layer. Continental crust (25-70 km, granitic) and oceanic crust (5-10 km, basaltic).
  • Mantle: Thick layer of silicate rock (2,900 km). Upper mantle contains the asthenosphere (partially molten).
  • Core: Iron-nickel. Outer core is liquid (2,200 km), inner core is solid (1,220 km radius) due to immense pressure.

Layers by Physical Properties

  • Lithosphere: Rigid crust + uppermost mantle; broken into tectonic plates.
  • Asthenosphere: Partially molten, flows slowly; plates glide over it.
  • Mesosphere: Lower mantle, stronger but still capable of slow flow.
